title: Gallery share reads decrypted temp-file URI without pinning — use-after-evict race during shareAsync
description: handleShare() resolves an opus-media URI to a decrypted temp file via decryptCache.getCached(mediaId, "full") and then awaits Sharing.shareAsync(shareUri, ...) without holding a pin. The only pin on that entry belongs to the rendering useDecryptedImage hook, which unpins on page unmount. Two realistic eviction windows exist while the share sheet is open: (1) FlatList windowing unmounts the source page if the user swipes during the share, dropping the pin and allowing LRU eviction (max 10 full-image entries) to delete the file; (2) selecting a share target that backgrounds the app triggers the AppState.background decrypted-temp-file sweep. Either deletes the file mid-share, so the OS share target receives a dead file:// path. Consequence is a failed/blank share (not data exposure — temp filenames are per-mediaId, no reuse), but it is a correctness hole in the security-sensitive decrypt-cache lifecycle that the pin API was built to close.
evidence: MediaGalleryViewer.tsx:146-159 — getCached() without pin(), then await Sharing.shareAsync(). mediaDecryptCache.ts:99-117 — pin/unpin refcount API exists; :171-175 LRU eviction skips only pinned entries. CLAUDE.md: decrypted temp files cleared on every app background transition.
suggestedFix: In handleShare, call decryptCache.pin(mediaId, "full") immediately after getCached() succeeds and decryptCache.unpin(mediaId, "full") in a finally block around the Sharing.shareAsync call, mirroring the useDecryptedImage hook's pin lifetime pattern.

title: Ungated console.error/console.warn calls in client/lib violate the Phase 6 __DEV__-gating invariant
description: client/lib/storage.ts contains ~15 console.error(...) sites (and other client/lib modules have a handful more) that are not __DEV__-gated. The Phase 6 security plan's cross-session invariant declares every console.* call in client/lib should be __DEV__-gated. The messages are generic (no PHI, no tokens), so practical risk is low, but production console output can carry stack traces / internal paths into device logs and the declared policy is not being met.
evidence: grep of console.error in client/lib/storage.ts — 15+ sites without __DEV__ guard (e.g. lines ~323, 384, 427, 447, 552, 562, 576, 590, 601, 621, 727, 739, 771, 793, 811).
suggestedFix: Sweep client/lib for ungated console.error/warn and wrap in `if (__DEV__)`. Keep it a guard sweep; a logger shim remains a separate decision.

title: POST/DELETE /api/push-tokens lack a per-user rate limit
description: Push-token registration endpoints require authentication and the upsert is idempotent on (userId, deviceId), so impact is low, but they are the only mutation surface without any limiter — an authenticated client can spam token writes. Informational hardening.
evidence: server/rateLimit.ts defines authRateLimiter, userSearchRateLimiter, invitationRateLimiter; no limiter mounted on push-token routes in routes.ts.
suggestedFix: Add a light per-user limiter (e.g. 30/min) to POST and DELETE /api/push-tokens reusing the existing express-rate-limit per-user keyGenerator pattern.

title: shell-quote critical advisory via react-native -> react-devtools-core (dev-tooling, non-breaking fix available)
description: npm audit --omit=dev now reports 1 critical: shell-quote ('quote() does not escape newlines in object .op values') reached via react-native -> react-devtools-core. react-devtools-core is development tooling that does not ship in the production JS bundle, so practical runtime risk is negligible, but the advisory sits in the prod dependency graph and has a non-breaking fix.
evidence: npm ls shell-quote: react-native@0.81.5 -> react-devtools-core@6.1.5 -> shell-quote@1.8.3. npm audit --omit=dev metadata: {critical:1, high:3, moderate:21}.
suggestedFix: Cleared by the same non-force `npm audit fix` wave as F003.

title: [CARRIED 2026-06-04] drizzle-orm 0.39.3 HIGH SQL-injection advisory — exploitability re-verified LOW this audit
description: Still open. This audit performed the full server/storage.ts identifier review the smoke report requested: zero sql.raw, zero sql.identifier, zero dynamic orderBy/table selection across 185+ Drizzle operations; the single sql template literal (tokenVersion increment) uses a static column reference. Practical exploitability confirmed low; fix remains a deliberate semver-major bump to >=0.45.2 with scratch-DB drizzle-kit push + server test suite.
evidence: package.json still pins drizzle-orm ^0.39.3; storage.ts grep for sql.raw/sql.identifier/dynamic orderBy returns nothing.
suggestedFix: Phase 2 (human-gated): bump to 0.45.x, tsc + server __tests__, drizzle-kit push against scratch DB, local endpoint smoke. Deploy is a separate decision.

suggestedFix: Per smoke-audit Cluster C plan; land before the dead-code deletion wave.

title: [CARRIED 2026-06-04] Dead no-op queueMicrotask block in allocateEpisodeSequence (also F007/F009)
description: Still open. The block allocates a throwaway Promise per call; the equality check against _episodeSequenceLocks always fails so no cleanup ever occurs. Consolidates smoke findings F006/F007/F009.
suggestedFix: Delete the block, keep only release() in finally. Land after F002 tests.

title: [CARRIED 2026-06-04] @xmldom/xmldom HIGH chain — build tooling only, track-only
description: Still open by design: confined to @bacons/apple-targets / @expo build tooling, not the shipped binary. Await upstream bump.
evidence: npm audit --omit=dev: 3 high all in @bacons/xcode -> @expo/plist -> @xmldom/xmldom chain.
